description:
You are acting as the on-call database administrator for an in-house PostgreSQL cluster.  
A recent performance review showed that the query optimiser is disabled and the
connection pool is too small.  Replication needs turning on as well.  
All configuration files are kept under

    /home/user/db_configs/

Two files already exist there and must be edited **in-place**:

1. /home/user/db_configs/db_settings.yaml  
2. /home/user/db_configs/replication.toml  

A. YAML file details (current state)

database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  pool:
    size: 10
    timeout: 300

queries:
  optimizer:
    enable: false
    cost_threshold: 100


Required YAML final state

database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  pool:
    size: 20
    timeout: 300

queries:
  optimizer:
    enable: true
    cost_threshold: 50


B. TOML file details (current state)

[replication]
enabled = false
max_replicas = 2

[maintenance]
vacuum = "daily"


Required TOML final state

[replication]
enabled = true
max_replicas = 3

[maintenance]
vacuum = "daily"


C. Produce an **audit log**  
Create a new text file /home/user/db_configs/optimization_log.txt summarising every
change you made.  
Use exactly the five lines shown below (no extra lines, no trailing spaces, no
blank line at the end of the file).  
The timestamp **must appear exactly as the literal string** 2023-01-01 00:00:00.

2023-01-01 00:00:00 - db_settings.yaml - queries.optimizer.enable - false -> true
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - db_settings.yaml - queries.optimizer.cost_threshold - 100 -> 50
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - db_settings.yaml - database.pool.size - 10 -> 20
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - replication.toml - replication.enabled - false -> true
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - replication.toml - replication.max_replicas - 2 -> 3

Your task:
1. Open a terminal in the working directory (you start in /home/user).
2. Navigate to the configuration directory, inspect both files, and apply the
   required edits so their contents match exactly the “Required … final state”
   blocks above.
3. Create the optimization_log.txt file with the exact five log lines given.
4. Verify your work by re-opening both configuration files and the log file.

Hints:
• You may use command-line editors or utilities such as sed, awk, yq, or
  toml-specific tools—your choice.  
• Only 3-4 well-chosen commands are necessary.  
• Make sure there are no extra quotation marks or indentation changes other
  than those explicitly required.  
• Do not create, rename or move any other files.

Automation notes (what the grader will check):
• File /home/user/db_configs/db_settings.yaml exists and matches the required YAML
  block exactly.  
• File /home/user/db_configs/replication.toml exists and matches the required
  TOML block exactly.  
• File /home/user/db_configs/optimization_log.txt exists and contains exactly the
  five lines shown above.

  port: 5432

    size: 20
    timeout: 300

    enable: true
    cost_threshold: 50

max_replicas = 3

[maintenance]
vacuum = "daily"


Required final content of /home/user/db_configs/optimization_log.txt (exactly 5 lines, newline only between lines, no trailing newline after last line):

2023-01-01 00:00:00 - db_settings.yaml - queries.optimizer.enable - false -> true
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - db_settings.yaml - queries.optimizer.cost_threshold - 100 -> 50
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - db_settings.yaml - database.pool.size - 10 -> 20
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - replication.toml - replication.enabled - false -> true
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - replication.toml - replication.max_replicas - 2 -> 3
